items.notes: Pronounced 'PU-nai-nen'. One of the most common color words. Declines like other -inen adjectives (punaista, punaisessa).
examples: Punainen auto on nopea. — The red car is fast.
Minulla on punainen takki. — I have a red jacket.

items.notes: Pronounced 'SI-ni-nen'. The color of the Finnish flag cross. Declines: sinistä, sinisessä.
examples: Taivas on sininen. — The sky is blue.
Suomen lippu on sinivalkoinen. — The Finnish flag is blue and white.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VIH-re-ä'. Note the ä ending. Does not follow the -inen pattern. Partitive: vihreää.
examples: Ruoho on vihreää. — The grass is green.
Haluan vihreän salaatin. — I want a green salad.

items.notes: Pronounced 'KEL-tai-nen'. Follows the -inen adjective pattern. Partitive: keltaista.
examples: Aurinko on keltainen. — The sun is yellow.
Keltaiset kukat ovat kauniita. — The yellow flowers are beautiful.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VAL-koi-nen'. The other color of the Finnish flag. Partitive: valkoista.
examples: Lumi on valkoista. — The snow is white.
Hänellä on valkoinen paita. — He/she has a white shirt.

items.notes: Pronounced 'MUS-ta'. Short and easy to remember. Does not follow -inen pattern. Partitive: mustaa.
examples: Kissa on musta. — The cat is black.
Juon mustaa kahvia. — I drink black coffee.

items.notes: Pronounced 'HAR-maa'. Note the double 'a'. Partitive: harmaata.
examples: Taivas on harmaa tänään. — The sky is grey today.
Harmaat pilvet tulevat. — The grey clouds are coming.

items.notes: Pronounced 'RUS-ke-a'. Note the 'ea' ending (not ä). Partitive: ruskeaa.
examples: Koira on ruskea. — The dog is brown.
Haluan ruskeat kengät. — I want brown shoes.

items.notes: Pronounced 'O-rans-si'. Borrowed from Swedish/French. Note double 's'. Partitive: oranssia.
examples: Appelsiini on oranssi. — The orange (fruit) is orange.
Auringonlasku on oranssi. — The sunset is orange.

items.notes: Pronounced 'vi-o-LET-ti'. Loanword. Note double 't'. Also 'purppura' exists but violetti is more common in everyday use.
examples: Violetit kukat tuoksuvat hyvältä. — The violet flowers smell good.
Tykkään violetista väristä. — I like the violet color.

items.notes: Literally 'light red' (vaalea + punainen). Pronounced 'VAA-le-an-PU-nai-nen'. A compound adjective. Sometimes shortened to 'pinkki' in casual speech.
examples: Vaaleanpunainen ruusu on kaunis. — The pink rose is beautiful.
Hänellä on vaaleanpunainen mekko. — She has a pink dress.

items.notes: Compound: tumma (dark) + sininen (blue). Pronounced 'TUM-man-SI-ni-nen'. The pattern 'tumman-' + color works for any dark shade.
examples: Meri on tummansininen. — The sea is dark blue.
Hänellä on tummansininen puku. — He/she has a dark blue suit.

items.notes: Compound: vaalea (light) + sininen (blue). Pronounced 'VAA-le-an-SI-ni-nen'. The pattern 'vaalean-' + color works for any light shade.
examples: Vauvan huone on vaaleansininen. — The baby's room is light blue.
Taivas on vaaleansininen kesällä. — The sky is light blue in summer.

items.notes: Pronounced 'KUL-tai-nen'. Derived from 'kulta' (gold). Follows -inen pattern. Also used figuratively: 'kultainen hetki' (golden moment).
examples: Kultainen sormus on pöydällä. — The golden ring is on the table.
Syksy tuo kultaiset lehdet. — Autumn brings golden leaves.

items.notes: Technically a noun (the metal) used as an adjective for color. Pronounced 'HO-pe-a'. The adjective form 'hopeinen' also exists but 'hopea' is common for color.
examples: Hopea auto kiiltää auringossa. — The silver car shines in the sun.
Hänellä on hopeanväriset korvakorut. — She has silver-colored earrings.

items.notes: Pronounced 'TUR-koo-si'. Loanword, easy to recognize. Used for the blue-green shade.
examples: Turkoosia vettä on Lapissa. — There is turquoise water in Lapland.
Turkoosi on kaunis väri. — Turquoise is a beautiful color.

items.notes: Pronounced 'BEEZH' (French pronunciation maintained). Indeclinable in Finnish — the form stays the same regardless of case.
examples: Beige sohva sopii olohuoneeseen. — A beige sofa suits the living room.
Seinät ovat beige. — The walls are beige.

items.notes: Pronounced 'VAA-le-a'. Used as a modifier before colors: 'vaaleanvihreä' (light green). Also means 'fair' for hair/skin. Opposite: 'tumma' (dark).
examples: Hänellä on vaaleat hiukset. — He/she has light (blond) hair.
Haluan vaalean vihreän paidan. — I want a light green shirt.
